info.title: SendKit API
info.description: The SendKit API allows you to send transactional emails programmatically.

post.description: Send a transactional email to a recipient. The `from` address must belong to a verified domain in your account. You must provide either `html` or `text` (or both).

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

example.name: validation_error
example.message: The from address domain is not verified.

example.name: rate_limit_exceeded
example.message: Rate limit exceeded. Please retry after 30 seconds.

post.description: Create a new contact or update an existing one if a contact with the same email already exists (upsert). You can optionally attach the contact to lists and set property values inline.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of contacts for your team. Results are ordered by creation date, newest first.
Retrieve a single contact by ID, including their lists and property values.

patch.description: Update a contact's fields. Only the provided fields will be updated. You can also sync lists and properties inline.

delete.description: Delete a contact by ID. This is a soft delete — the contact will no longer appear in API responses.

post.description: Create a new contact list for your team.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all contact lists for your team.
Retrieve a single contact list by ID.

delete.description: Delete a contact list by ID. Contacts in the list are not deleted.

409.description: Conflict — list is used by active campaigns or automations

example.message: This list is used by active campaigns and cannot be deleted.

post.description: Add a contact to one or more lists. Lists that the contact already belongs to will be ignored.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of lists that a contact belongs to.

200.description: A paginated list of lists the contact belongs to

delete.summary: Remove a contact from a list
delete.description: Remove a contact from a specific list. The contact itself is not deleted.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The list ID to remove the contact from.

post.description: Create a new contact property for your team. Properties define custom fields that can be set on contacts.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all contact properties for your team.

patch.description: Update a contact property's key, type, or fallback value.

delete.description: Delete a contact property by ID. All associated property values on contacts will also be removed.

409.description: Conflict — property is used in segment filters

example.message: This property is used in segment filters and cannot be deleted.

post.description: Add an email address to the suppression list. Suppressed emails will not receive any emails. If the email already exists, the existing entry is returned. The email is normalized to lowercase.

201.description: Suppression entry created or already exists

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all suppressed email addresses.

200.description: A paginated list of suppression entries

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

delete.description: Remove an email address from the suppression list. This allows the email to receive emails again.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

post.description: Validate an email address by checking syntax, DNS records, mailbox existence, and whether it is a disposable or role-based address.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

post.description: Add a new sending domain to your account. The domain will be created with DNS records that you need to configure at your DNS provider before verifying. Your account has a domain limit based on your plan.

403.description: Forbidden — domain limit reached for your plan

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all domains in your account, including their DNS records and verification status.
Retrieve a single domain by ID, including its DNS records and current verification status.

patch.description: Update domain settings such as open tracking, click tracking, and TLS mode. Changes to tracking options are synced with the email provider.

delete.description: Delete a domain from your account. The domain must not have any active senders. All associated DNS records and provider configurations will be cleaned up.

422.description: Domain has active senders and cannot be deleted

example.message: This domain has senders and cannot be deleted. Remove all senders first.

post.description: Trigger DNS verification for a domain. This checks that the required DNS records (DKIM, SPF, DMARC, MX) are properly configured. Verification runs asynchronously — poll the domain to check the updated status.

get.description: Returns the current status of the API. This endpoint does not require authentication.

bearerAuth.description: API key from your SendKit dashboard. Pass it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key

NotFound.description: The requested resource was not found

ValidationError.description: Validation error — invalid or missing parameters

from.description: Sender email address. Must belong to a verified domain. Accepts plain email or with display name.

html.description: HTML body of the email. Required if `text` is not provided.

text.description: Plain text body of the email. Required if `html` is not provided.

headers.description: Custom email headers as key-value pairs.

scheduled_at.description: Schedule the email for future delivery. Must be a future ISO 8601 timestamp.

content_type.description: MIME type of the file. If not provided, it will be inferred from the filename.

id.description: Unique identifier of the created email.

user_id.description: An external user identifier from your application.

properties.description: Custom property values. Keys are uppercased property names.

updated_at.description: When the contact was last updated.

from.description: The index of the first item on the current page.

per_page.description: The number of items per page.

to.description: The index of the last item on the current page.

path.description: The base URL for the paginated resource.

prev.description: URL to the previous page, or null if on the first page.

next.description: URL to the next page, or null if on the last page.

email.description: The contact's email address. If a contact with this email already exists, it will be updated.

user_id.description: An external user identifier from your application.

unsubscribed.description: Whether the contact should be marked as unsubscribed.

list_ids.description: List IDs to attach the contact to. Replaces existing list memberships.

properties.description: Property values to set on the contact. Keys must match existing property keys.

user_id.description: An external user identifier from your application.

unsubscribed.description: Whether the contact should be marked as unsubscribed.

list_ids.description: List IDs to attach the contact to. Replaces existing list memberships.

properties.description: Property values to set on the contact. Keys must match existing property keys.

updated_at.description: When the list was last updated.
When the list was last updated.

name.description: The new name of the list.

list_ids.description: IDs of the lists to add the contact to.

key.description: The property key. Used when setting values on contacts.

type.description: The data type of the property.

fallback_value.description: Default value used when a contact doesn't have this property set.

updated_at.description: When the property was last updated.

key.description: The property key. Must be unique per team.

type.description: The data type of the property.

fallback_value.description: Default value used when a contact doesn't have this property set.

type.description: The data type of the property.

fallback_value.description: Default value used when a contact doesn't have this property set.

status.description: The current verification status of the domain.

region.description: The AWS region where the domain is configured.

sending_enabled.description: Whether sending is enabled for this domain.

dns_records.description: The DNS records that need to be configured for this domain.

updated_at.description: When the domain was last updated.

status.description: The verification status of this DNS record.

priority.description: The priority of the DNS record (for MX records).

updated_at.description: When the record was last updated.

region.description: The AWS region for the domain.

open_tracking.description: Enable open tracking. Defaults to false.

click_tracking.description: Enable click tracking. Defaults to false.

tls.description: TLS delivery mode. Defaults to opportunistic.

message.example: The from address domain is not verified.

id.description: Unique identifier for the suppression entry.

reason.description: The reason the email was suppressed.

updated_at.description: When the entry was last updated.

email.description: The email address that was validated.

is_valid.description: Whether the email address is considered valid.

has_valid_syntax.description: Whether the email has valid syntax.

has_valid_dns.description: Whether the domain has valid DNS records.

mailbox_exists.description: Whether the mailbox exists on the server.

is_role_address.description: Whether this is a role-based address (e.g. info@, admin@).

is_disposable.description: Whether the domain is a disposable email provider.

is_random_input.description: Whether the local part appears to be randomly generated.

should_block.description: Whether this email should be blocked from receiving emails.

block_reason.description: The reason the email should be blocked, if applicable.
